title: Consolida raveyi
taxonomy: Consolida raveyi is classified within the family Papaveraceae. This species belongs to the genus Consolida, which is a group of annual flowering plants commonly known as larkspurs. Consolida raveyi is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is closely related to other species within the same genus. The classification of Consolida raveyi is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its distinctive flowers and seed pods. The exact classification of Consolida raveyi may also be subject to ongoing revisions and updates in botanical taxonomy.

side_effects: Consolida raveyi, also known as larkspur, is toxic to humans and livestock if ingested. intro: Consolida raveyi, also known as Larkspur, is a flowering plant belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Consolida raveyi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
